content.thinking:
O usuário quer verificar o status do projeto. De acordo com o protocolo do harness, preciso:

1. Ler `.harness/progress.txt` para entender o histórico de sessões
2. Ler `.harness/features.json` para ver o status das features

Vou começar lendo esses arquivos para dar um relatório completo ao usuário.

content.text: Vou verificar o status do projeto lendo os arquivos do harness.

Uses BlueprintJS Card with SVG heatmap rendering. Integrated into App.tsx with camera position from canvas state. Tested via Playwright MCP - all features verified working. 2025-12-17."

   999→      "passes": true,

  1016→        "rootCause": "The ctrlKey branch (trackpad pinch) was using accumulation (50ms timeout) + animation (250ms) which added ~300ms latency. Touch pinch (handlePinchZoom) applied zoom directly with no latency.",

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.1",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "parallelExecution": {
    "description": "Parallel execution configuration for remaining features (updated 2025-12-17)",
    "runner": "bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts",
    "worktreeDir": ".trees/",
    "status": "MOSTLY COMPLETE - Only 6 features remain",
    "waves": [
      {
        "wave": 1,
        "description": "Navigation enhancements + Task panel (can run in parallel)",
        "maxConcurrency": 3,
        "groups": [
          {
            "id": "group-A",
            "features": [
              "F016"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "Shift+Scroll vertical pan"
          },
          {
            "id": "group-B",
            "features": [
              "F026"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "App.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "Task details panel"
          },
          {
            "id": "group-C",
            "features": [
              "F027",
              "F028"
            ],
            "sequential": true,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "Keyboard shortcuts zoom/pan"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 2,
        "description": "Accessibility (depends on Wave 1)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 1,
        "groups": [
          {
            "id": "group-D",
            "features": [
              "F021"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx",
              "layers"
            ],
            "note": "Keyboard task navigation"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 3,
        "description": "Final accessibility (depends on F021)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 2,
        "groups": [
          {
            "id": "group-E",
            "features": [
              "F022"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx",
              "MicroLayer.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "ARIA labels"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "D3-MIGRATE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Integrate D3 modules to replace ad-hoc implementations (zoom, scales, time, arrays)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "research": "apps/canvas/d3-opportunities.txt",
      "rationale": "Comprehensive codebase analysis identified ~300 lines of ad-hoc implementations that can be replaced with battle-tested D3 modules. Benefits: cross-browser compatibility, maintenance reduction, potential BUG-009 fix via d3-zoom.",
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase1_quick_wins": {
          "description": "Replace simple utilities with D3 modules (LOW complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-array",
            "d3-time",
            "d3-time-format",
            "d3-scale",
            "d3-interpolate"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-array: bun add d3-array @types/d3-array",
            "types.ts: Replace calculateWorldBounds/calculateDataBounds with d3.extent()",
            "Install d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale: bun add d3-time d3-time-format d3-scale @types/d3-time @types/d3-time-format @types/d3-scale",
            "TimelineHeader.tsx: Replace addDays/getUnitStart/advanceUnit with d3.timeDay.offset()/floor()",
            "TimelineHeader.tsx: Replace formatDate with d3.timeFormat()",
            "Install d3-interpolate: bun add d3-interpolate @types/d3-interpolate",
            "Minimap.tsx: Replace worldToMinimap/minimapToWorld with d3.scaleLinear()",
            "Minimap.tsx: Replace interpolateColor with d3.interpolateRgb()"
          ],
          "estimatedReduction": "~120 lines"
        },
        "phase2_core_optimization": {
          "description": "Replace wheel event handling with d3-zoom (MEDIUM complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-zoom",
            "d3-selection"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-zoom, d3-selection: bun add d3-zoom d3-selection @types/d3-zoom @types/d3-selection",
            "Create useD3Zoom hook as adapter for R3F integration",
            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace normalizeWheelDelta() with d3-zoom wheelDelta",
            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace trackpad detection heuristics with d3-zoom built-in",
            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace PinchState with d3-zoom touchable()",
            "Verify BUG-009 (trackpad zoom lag) is resolved",
            "Cross-browser testing: Chrome, Safari, Firefox"
          ],
          "estimatedReduction": "~100 lines",
          "resolvesBug": "BUG-009"
        },
        "phase3_visual_polish": {
          "description": "Replace heatmap generation with D3 (LOW complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-scale-chromatic"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-scale-chromatic: bun add d3-scale-chromatic @types/d3-scale-chromatic",
            "MacroLayer.tsx: Replace calculateDensityGrid with d3.bin() + d3.rollup()",
            "MacroLayer.tsx: Replace getHeatmapColor with d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateYlOrRd)"
          ],
          "estimatedReduction": "~50 lines"
        }
      },
      "bundleImpact": {
        "totalGzipped": "~26KB",
        "modules": {
          "d3-zoom": "~8KB",
          "d3-scale": "~6KB",
          "d3-array": "~4KB",
          "d3-time": "~3KB",
          "d3-time-format": "~2KB",
          "d3-interpolate": "~3KB"
        }
      },
      "successCriteria": [
        "All unit tests pass",
        "Visual verification via Playwright MCP",
        "No console errors",
        "60fps maintained during pan/zoom",
        "Bundle size increase < 30KB gzipped"
      ],
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Phase 1: d3-array, d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale, d3-interpolate integrated in types.ts, TimelineHeader.tsx, Minimap.tsx. Phase 2 (d3-zoom) deferred: BUG-009 already fixed, R3F adapter complexity not justified. Phase 3: d3-scale-chromatic for MacroLayer heatmap. Total ~120 lines replaced with battle-tested D3 modules.",
      "completedDate": "2025-12-17"
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-002",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - Only render/process entities visible in viewport",
      "passes": true,
      "completedDate": "2025-12-17",
      "blockedBy": null,
      "research": "docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md",
      "rationale": "Current InstancedMesh sends all 60k instances to GPU regardless of viewport. This wastes GPU bandwidth and CPU cycles. Implementing viewport culling with R-tree spatial index can reduce rendered instances from 60k to ~500 (visible only), dramatically improving performance on mobile devices.",
      "relatedBugs": [
        "BUG-009"
      ],
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase0_trackpad_zoom_fix": {
          "description": "Fix trackpad zoom jank before spatial optimizations (BUG-009)",
          "rationale": "User reported smooth minimap navigation but laggy trackpad zoom. This is an input event handling issue, NOT a rendering issue. Must be fixed first as it affects perceived performance regardless of culling.",
          "tasks": [
            "Profile trackpad zoom events in Chrome DevTools (Performance tab)",
            "Identify if bottleneck is animation (250ms) or accumulation timeout (50ms)",
            "Option A: Remove animation for trackpad - apply zoom directly like minimap",
            "Option B: Update target during animation instead of starting new animation",
            "Option C: Replace custom implementation with d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react",
            "Test on MacBook Pro Magic Trackpad specifically",
            "Ensure smooth 60fps during rapid pinch-to-zoom gestures"
          ],
          "marketSolutions": {
            "d3-zoom": "Industry standard, handles wheel/touch normalization, momentum",
            "use-gesture": "@use-gesture/react - React hooks for gestures, used by react-three/drei",
            "leva-zoom": "Study how leva/drei implement zoom for R3F"
          }
        },
        "phase1_spatial_interface": {
          "description": "Define spatial query INTERFACE (implementation deferred to STATE-ARCH-001)",
          "rationale": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays. Here we only define the interface and use a simple O(n) filter as placeholder. This avoids duplicate work.",
          "tasks": [
            "Create ISpatialIndex interface at apps/canvas/src/spatial/types.ts",
            "Interface: getVisibleTasks(viewportBounds) → taskIds[]",
            "Create SimpleSpatialIndex with O(n) filter (temporary placeholder)",
            "SimpleSpatialIndex uses existing object-based task array",
            "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 will replace with RBush + TypedArrays"
          ],
          "deferredTo": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 - Full RBush implementation with dirty flagging"
        },
        "phase2_culled_instanced_mesh": {
          "description": "Implement per-instance culling for InstancedMesh",
          "tasks": [
            "Create CulledInstancedMesh wrapper at apps/canvas/src/rendering/CulledInstancedMesh.ts",
            "Implement instance reordering: visible instances at front, hidden at back",
            "Use mesh.count to render only visible instances (single draw call)",
            "Track dirty regions for partial buffer updates (avoid full re-upload)",
            "Pre-allocate reorder buffers to avoid GC in hot path",
            "Benchmark: Update time should be <1ms for 1000 visible instances"
          ]
        },
        "phase3_layer_integration": {
          "description": "Integrate culling with existing LOD layers",
          "tasks": [
            "Update MesoLayer to use CulledInstancedMesh + SpatialIndex",
            "Update MacroLayer to use viewport-based heatmap aggregation",
            "Verify MicroLayer already uses filterVisibleTasks (improve with R-tree)",
            "Add viewportBounds calculation in InfiniteCanvas",
            "Pass viewportBounds to all layers",
            "Ensure culling works during pan/zoom animations"
          ]
        },
        "phase4_validation": {
          "description": "Performance validation and profiling",
          "tasks": [
            "Create performance test suite with __PERF_START_SESSION__",
            "Measure FPS during rapid pan/zoom with all 60k tasks",
            "Profile with Chrome DevTools - verify reduced GPU work",
            "Test on mobile devices (iOS Safari, Chrome Android)",
            "Document before/after metrics in PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md"
          ]
        }
      },
      "performanceTargets": {
        "viewportQuery": "<5ms for 1000 results",
        "instanceUpdate": "<1ms for 1000 visible",
        "fps": "60fps constant during pan/zoom",
        "gpuInstances": "Only visible count (not all 60k)"
      },
      "libraries": {
        "spatialIndex": "RBush (R-tree) - https://github.com/mourner/rbush",
        "alternative": "Flatbush (static, 5-8x faster if data rarely changes)"
      },
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Foundation infrastructure for viewport culling implemented. Phase 0: BUG-009 trackpad zoom fixed. Phase 1: Created ISpatialIndex interface + SimpleSpatialIndex (O(n) placeholder) in apps/canvas/src/spatial/. Phase 2: Created useCulledInstances, calculateViewportBounds, useStableViewportBounds hooks in apps/canvas/src/rendering/. Phase 3: Integrated spatialIndex into InfiniteCanvas → LODLayers → MesoLayer (props plumbing). Phase 4: Performance validated at 120fps. DEFERRED: Actual culling optimization (mesh.count) to STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 when RBush replaces SimpleSpatialIndex. 2025-12-17."
    },
    {
      "id": "STATE-ARCH-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Refatorar arquitetura de state management para padrão Dual-Store de alta performance",
      "passes": true,
      "completedDate": "2025-12-17",
      "blockedBy": "PERF-002",
      "research": "docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf",
      "rationale": "Documento de pesquisa identifica GC como gargalo principal em aplicações WebGL móveis. React Hooks e stores reativos são inadequados para updates a 60fps. Propõe arquitetura Dual-Store separando Simulation State (TypedArrays/ECS) de Application State (@zazcart/tools state-machine). NOTA: Usamos @zazcart/tools (packages/toolkit) para UI state, NUNCA Zustand.",
      "implementationPlan": {

            "Criar Views/Cursors para acesso ergonômico aos dados tipados",

            "Benchmark: 100k entidades devem ocupar ~800KB vs dezenas de MB com objetos JS"

          "clarification": "Este store é para SIMULATION STATE (dados de entidades a 60fps). NÃO usa @zazcart/tools. Acesso direto, mutação direta, máxima performance."

            "Adaptar para trabalhar com TypedArrays do Phase1 (não objetos JS)",
            "Otimizar: bulk loading em startup, rebuild apenas em create/delete (não em move)",
            "Implementar dirty flagging para minimizar recálculos",
            "Substituir SimpleSpatialIndex (placeholder PERF-002) por RBushSpatialIndex",
            "Target: queries de viewport com 1000 resultados em <5ms"

        "phase3_ui_state_bridge": {
          "description": "UI State via @zazcart/tools + Bridge com Simulation State",

            "Criar slice 'interaction' com selectedEntityId, hoveredEntityId",

            "Bridge: Scheduler sincroniza Simulation→UI a 30fps máximo (throttled)",
            "UI NUNCA acessa TypedArrays diretamente - apenas via bridge/selectors"

          "description": "Implementar Sync Loop centralizado (ordem fixa de execução)",
          "tasks": [
            "Criar scheduler com ordem fixa: processInput() → updateSimulation() → renderCanvas() → syncUI()",
            "updateSimulation opera em TypedArrays (Simulation State)",
            "syncUI opera em @zazcart/tools (UI State) - throttled a 30fps",
            "Desacoplar physics/simulation tick de render tick para determinismo",
            "requestAnimationFrame como driver principal do loop"
          ]

        "phase5_object_pooling_gc_elimination": {
          "description": "Eliminar pressão de GC no hot path",
          "tasks": [

            "Criar ResultPool para arrays de resultados de queries",
            "REGRA: Zero 'new Object()' no requestAnimationFrame loop",
            "REGRA: Evitar closures em funções chamadas por frame (closures capturam escopo)",
            "REGRA: Preferir 'for' loops sobre forEach/map/filter (criam contexto de função)",
            "REGRA: Pré-computar valores constantes FORA do loop de renderização",
            "Validar via Chrome DevTools Memory tab - zero GC spikes durante pan/zoom"
          ]

            "Implementar LOD mais agressivo para tiles fora do centro do viewport",

          "wheelAccumulatorRef.current.timeout clears/resets too frequently",

        ],

      }
    },

  },

   501→## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅
   502→
   503→### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
   504→### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED
   505→
   506→**Objective:**
   507→Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.
   508→
   509→**Research Conducted:**
   510→- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
   511→- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
   512→- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
   513→- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)
   514→
   515→**Key Findings:**
   516→
   517→1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
   518→   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
   519→   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling
   520→
   521→2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
   522→   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
   523→   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
   524→   - Perfect for viewport culling queries
   525→
   526→3. **Culling Strategy:**
   527→   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
   528→   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
   529→   - No new allocations during pan/zoom
   530→
   531→4. **Alternative Libraries:**
   532→   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
   533→   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
   534→   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data
   535→
   536→**Documents Created:**
   537→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings
   538→
   539→**Task Created: PERF-002**
   540→
   541→Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:
   542→
   543→1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
   544→2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
   545→3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
   546→4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling
   547→
   548→**Dependency Update:**
   549→- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
   550→- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
   551→- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon
   552→
   553→**Performance Targets:**
   554→- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
   555→- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
   556→- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
   557→- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)
   558→
   559→**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
   560→- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
   561→- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
   562→- Root cause candidates:
   563→  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
   564→  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
   565→  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
   566→- Solution options:
   567→  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
   568→  - Option B: Update target during animation
   569→  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)
   570→

   572→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)
   573→- `features.json`:
   574→  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)
   575→  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation
   576→  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002
   577→- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)

   581→- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder

   931→## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅
   932→
   933→### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE
   934→
   935→**Objective:**
   936→Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.
   937→
   938→**Research:**
   939→- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   940→- Researched git worktree patterns for AI agent parallel development
   941→- Analyzed file conflict potential for all 10 pending features
   942→
   943→**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**
   944→
   945→```
   946→WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
   947→├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
   948→├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
   949→└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
   950→
   951→WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):
   952→├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
   953→└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]
   954→
   955→WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - 1 group):
   956→└── Group F: F021 (Keyboard task navigation) → Multiple files [HIGH risk]
   957→
   958→WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
   959→└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
   960→```
   961→
   962→**Files Created:**
   963→- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
   964→- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
   965→- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation
   966→
   967→**Files Modified:**
   968→- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
   969→- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration
   970→
   971→**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
   972→1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
   973→2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
   974→3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
   975→4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
   976→5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
   977→6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
   978→7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
   979→8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees
   980→
   981→**Speedup Calculation:**
   982→- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
   983→- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
   984→- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**
   985→
   986→**Usage:**
   987→```bash
   988→# Show status
   989→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status
   990→
   991→# Execute Wave 1
   992→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1
   993→
   994→# Merge completed feature
   995→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
   996→```

  1538→### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE
  1539→
  1540→**Objective:**
  1541→Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.
  1542→
  1543→**Analysis:**
  1544→Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:
  1545→
  1546→1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
  1547→   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
  1548→   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
  1549→   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌
  1550→
  1551→2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
  1552→   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
  1553→   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
  1554→   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
  1555→   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
  1556→   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3
  1557→
  1558→3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
  1559→   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
  1560→   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)
  1561→
  1562→**Solution:**
  1563→Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
  1564→- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
  1565→- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
  1566→- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

  1593→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
  1594→
  1595→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
  1596→
  1597→**Problem:**
  1598→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
  1599→
  1600→**Root Cause Analysis:**
  1601→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
  1602→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
  1603→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
  1604→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
  1605→
  1606→**Solution Implemented:**
  1607→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
  1608→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
  1609→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
  1610→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
  1611→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
  1612→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
  1613→   - `handlePinchZoom`
  1614→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
  1615→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
  1616→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
  1617→
  1618→**Files Changed:**
  1619→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
  1620→- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)
  1621→
  1622→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
  1623→- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
  1624→- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
  1625→- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
  1626→- ✅ No console errors

  1652→

  1661→- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
  1662→- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)

  1666→
  1667→### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE
  1668→
  1669→**Objective:**
  1670→Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.
  1671→
  1672→**Implementation:**
  1673→
  1674→1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
  1675→   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
  1676→   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
  1677→   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly
  1678→
  1679→2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
  1680→   - Track event frequency with timestamps
  1681→   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
  1682→   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
  1683→   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode
  1684→
  1685→3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
  1686→   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
  1687→   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)
  1688→
  1689→4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
  1690→   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
  1691→   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events
  1692→
  1693→**Constants Added:**
  1694→```typescript
  1695→LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
  1696→PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
  1697→MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
  1698→TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
  1699→MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
  1700→TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
  1701→TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
  1702→```
  1703→

## Session: 2025-12-17 (BUG-005 + BUG-009 Fixes) - COMPLETE ✅

### Bug Fixes Completed

**BUG-005: Date Labels Not Visible in TimelineHeader - FIXED**

Problem: Date labels in TimelineHeader were not visible at certain zoom levels (especially MACRO mode).

Root Cause:
1. Gradient overlays (30px width) covered labels due to missing z-index
2. PerformancePanel positioned at `top: 50` overlapped TimelineHeader

Fix:
- Added `zIndex: 2` to labels and background color for readability
- Reduced gradient overlay width from 30px to 20px with `zIndex: 1`
- Moved PerformancePanel from "top-left" to "bottom-left" position

**BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance Issue - FIXED**

Problem: Trackpad pinch-to-zoom on MacBook had ~300ms latency causing perceived lag.

Root Cause:
- 50ms accumulation timeout to batch wheel events
- 250ms animation duration per zoom operation
- Multiple rapid pinches queued animations

Fix:
- Modified `handleWheel` ctrlKey branch to apply zoom DIRECTLY
- Removed accumulation timeout for trackpad pinch events
- Removed animation delay - zoom applied immediately like `handlePinchZoom`
- Cursor-centered zoom preserved (world point stays fixed)

Also refactored handleWheel to Google Maps-like behavior:
- 2-finger scroll = pan (deltaX + deltaY)
- Ctrl+scroll (pinch) = zoom toward cursor

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` - BUG-005 fix
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` - PerformancePanel position change
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - BUG-009 fix
- `features.json` - Marked BUG-005 and BUG-009 as passes: true

**Commits:**
- `4796878` fix(ui): BUG-005 - date labels now visible in TimelineHeader at all zoom levels
- `910a44b` fix(perf): BUG-009 - trackpad zoom now applies instantly without lag

**Status:** Both bugs fixed and verified via Playwright MCP visual testing.

### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management - PLANNING COMPLETE

- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)

**Task Updated: STATE-ARCH-001**

Comprehensive 8-phase implementation plan:

7. **Phase 7 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches
8. **Phase 8 - Worker Layer:** Optional, OffscreenCanvas + SharedArrayBuffer

**Critical Decision: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**

PDF recommends Zustand, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** exclusively:
- Documented in 3 places with "NUNCA Zustand" warning
- Clarifications added to phases explaining Simulation vs UI state separation
- @zazcart/tools is ONLY for UI state (<30fps, event-driven)
- Simulation State uses TypedArrays with direct access (60fps)

## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED

**Objective:**
Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.

**Research Conducted:**
- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)

**Key Findings:**

1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling

2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
   - Perfect for viewport culling queries

3. **Culling Strategy:**
   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
   - No new allocations during pan/zoom

4. **Alternative Libraries:**
   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data

**Documents Created:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings

**Task Created: PERF-002**

Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling

**Dependency Update:**
- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon

**Performance Targets:**
- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)

**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
- Root cause candidates:
  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
- Solution options:
  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
  - Option B: Update target during animation
  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)

**Files Changed:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)
- `features.json`:
  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)
  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation
  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002
- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)

**Alignment Fix (Added):**

- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder

## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management Architecture - PLANNED

**Objective:**
Plan refactoring of state management architecture based on research document analyzing Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS, and WebGL game engines.

**Research Document:**
- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf`
- Key finding: GC (Garbage Collection) is the primary bottleneck, not rendering
- React Hooks and reactive stores inadequate for 60fps on mobile
- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)

**Task Created: STATE-ARCH-001**

Added comprehensive task to features.json with 5 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Foundation:** Create EntityStore with TypedArrays (Structure of Arrays pattern)
2. **Phase 2 - Spatial Index:** Implement RBush R-tree for viewport queries
3. **Phase 3 - UI Bridge:** Connect Engine State to @zazcart/tools (NOT Zustand as PDF suggests)
4. **Phase 4 - Object Pooling:** Eliminate GC pauses in animation loops
5. **Phase 5 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches

**CRITICAL DECISION: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**

The PDF recommends Zustand for UI state, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** (packages/toolkit):
- Already implemented and integrated
- Uses Immer for immutability
- Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous updates
- Has slice-based architecture matching project needs

**Files Changed:**
- `features.json` - Added STATE-ARCH-001 task with detailed implementation plan
- `CLAUDE.md` - Added "High-Performance Architecture" section with guidelines

**Performance Targets:**
- 100,000+ entities renderable
- 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- <100MB memory for 100k entities
- <5ms viewport queries
- Zero perceptible GC pauses

**Next Steps:**
- Implementation will be phased over multiple sessions
- Start with Phase 1 (EntityStore) when prioritized

- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration

**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees

**Speedup Calculation:**
- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**

## Session: 2025-12-16 (PERF-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.

**Implementation:**

1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders

2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows

3. **Global Console API:**
   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

4. **Session Statistics:**
   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   - MS: avg, max frame time
   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   - Duration and total frames

5. **UI Integration:**
   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   - "Perf" button in navbar
   - Panel positioned top-right
   - Runs in development mode by default

**Known Issue:**
- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

**F117 Archived:**
- "Full visual parity with POC reference" no longer relevant
- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX

### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.

**Implementation:**

- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**

- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

---

### Para Executar

```bash
cd apps/canvas && bun run dev
# Abre em http://localhost:5173
```

O projeto está completo, mas há fases adiadas do STATE-ARCH-001:
- Phase 4: Sync Loop Scheduler
- Phase 5: Object Pooling & GC Elimination
- Phase 6: Mobile Optimizations

content.content:
1→Part 1: The "Anti-Hooks" Doctrine
     2→
     3→Here is the refined instruction and explanation for your documentation/chat context.
     4→
     5→Instruction:
     6→> "Strictly separate Application State (Data/Logic) from View State (UI). Do NOT use useState or useReducer for application logic, business
     7→rules, or high-frequency data (e.g., game loops, real-time coords). Use them only for ephemeral UI states (e.g., 'isMenuOpen', 'hoverState'). All
     8→shared/persistent state must live in the @zazcart/tools store. Components must read state via useSelector (reactive) or direct store access
     9→(imperative/animation loops)."
    10→
    11→The Technical "Why" (Succinct Explanation):
    12→React Hooks (useState) couple your data's lifecycle to the Component's render cycle. This is fatal for high-performance or complex apps because:
    13→1. The "Stale Closure" Trap: React components are functions that capture variables at a specific moment in time. In a requestAnimationFrame loop
    14→   (running at 60fps), a closure created by useEffect or useCallback often sees "old" data from the previous render, causing logic bugs that are
    15→   nearly impossible to debug.
    16→2. Async/Batching De-sync: React state updates are asynchronous and batched. If you set state in Frame A, you might not read it back until Frame
    17→   B. In game logic, you need the value immediately in the same tick.
    18→3. Performance Checkmate: To fix stale closures, you add dependencies to useEffect. This forces the component to re-render constantly just to
    19→   "refresh" the closure, killing performance.
    20→4. Serialization: Hooks state is locked inside the component tree. You cannot easily snapshot it, persist it to local storage, or send it over a
    21→   WebSocket for multiplayer sync without writing complex "extraction" logic. External stores are serialized by default.
    22→
    23→  ---
    24→
    25→Part 2: Critical Audit of @zazcart/tools State Machine
    26→
    27→I have analyzed your code in packages/toolkit/src/state-machine.
    28→Verdict: It is a robust, feature-rich library for Business Apps, but it has critical performance bottlenecks for High-Frequency 3D/Game
    29→scenarios.
    30→
    31→Here is the analysis of 95% of the aspects involved.
    32→
    33→1. Architecture & Design
    34→* The Good:
    35→    * Architecture: The "Slice" pattern combined with useSyncExternalStore is the correct modern approach.
    36→    * Dev Experience: Usage of Immer simplifies reducer logic significantly.
    37→    * Hydration: The built-in Cache and Metadata strategies are excellent for "Resume where I left off" features in business apps.
    38→* The Bad (Architecture):
    39→    * Over-abstraction: You have built a wrapper around Redux principles but added significant runtime overhead for features (like Metadata)
    40→      that are not always needed.
    41→
    42→2. Performance Bottlenecks (The "Killer" issues)
    43→* Garbage Collection Thrashing (`machine.ts`):
    44→    * Problem: The UpsertStateMetadata function runs on every single action dispatch. It spreads a new object: [METADATA_FIELD_NAME]: {
    45→      ...previousMetadata, changes: changes + 1, ... }.
    46→    * Impact: In a 3D game dragging an object (dispatching MOVE 60 times/sec), you are allocating 60 new metadata objects per second, plus the
    47→      root state object. This will trigger the Garbage Collector frequently, causing "stutters" (frame drops).
    48→* Immer Overhead:
    49→    * Problem: You use produce (Immer) inside RootReducer and effectively wrap selectors in GetSelectorsWrapper.
    50→    * Impact: Immer is fast, but it uses Proxies. Wrapping/Unwrapping proxies 60 times a second adds measurable overhead compared to raw object
    51→      spread or direct mutation (in controlled environments).
    52→* Runtime Validation (`context.ts`):
    53→    * Problem: In context.ts, the Handler.get() calls check() which calls Assert which validates a schema.
    54→    * Code: runtimeActions in hooks.ts calls getContext() on every dispatch.
    55→    * Impact: Every time you dispatch an action, you are paying the cost of a schema validation check. This is unnecessary CPU waste in
    56→      production.
    57→
    58→3. Specific Code Issues & Bugs
    59→* The "Hybrid Object" Anti-Pattern (`hooks.ts`):
    60→    * Line: return Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection);
    61→    * Analysis: You are returning an Array that is also an Object (with properties).
    62→    * Why it's bad: V8 (Chrome's JS engine) hates this. It creates a complex "shape" that de-optimizes property access. It makes JIT compilation
    63→      harder. Just return an object { state, actions } or a tuple [state, actions]. Don't mix them.
    64→* Double Proxying (`machine.ts`):
    65→    * Code: GetSelectorsWrapper uses produce(state, ...) to inject selector values.
    66→    * Analysis: You are running Immer after the reducer has already run. You are double-processing the state tree.
    67→* Selector Equality:
    68→    * Analysis: Your store notifies listeners on any change. useSyncExternalStore handles the "select" logic, but if you have expensive
    69→      selectors derived from state (e.g., filtering a list of 10,000 items), your current implementation re-runs the selector on every dispatch
    70→      of any action, even if the relevant slice didn't change. You lack "memoized selectors" (like reselect).
    71→
    72→4. Recommendation for your 3D Game Use Case
    73→
    74→Your current library is too heavy for the onUpdate / useFrame loop of a 3D engine.
    75→
    76→Immediate Fixes for `packages/toolkit`:
    77→1. Bypass Validation: Add a flag to disable context.ts schema validation in production (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production').
    78→2. Opt-out Metadata: Allow creating a machine with metadata: false. A MOUSE_MOVE action should not update updatedAt timestamps or change
    79→   counters.
    80→3. Optimize Hooks: Change the return type of useMachine to a standard object or tuple. Remove Object.assign magic.
    81→
    82→Alternative Approach:
    83→For the 3D transforms, do not use this library's dispatch loop.
    84→* Keep: User Profile, Inventory, UI State in this library.
    85→* Discard: Mouse coordinates, Camera rotation, Object positions. Use a raw Float32Array or a simple ref based mutable store (like
    86→  valtio/vanilla) for those specific high-frequency values to bypass the Redux/Immer/Metadata overhead completely.
    87→
    88→Summary
    89→The library is "Enterprise Grade" (good for reliable business logic, hydration, caching) but "Game Engine Unfriendly" (allocates too much memory
    90→per tick).

file.content:
Part 1: The "Anti-Hooks" Doctrine

Here is the refined instruction and explanation for your documentation/chat context.

Instruction:
> "Strictly separate Application State (Data/Logic) from View State (UI). Do NOT use useState or useReducer for application logic, business
rules, or high-frequency data (e.g., game loops, real-time coords). Use them only for ephemeral UI states (e.g., 'isMenuOpen', 'hoverState'). All
shared/persistent state must live in the @zazcart/tools store. Components must read state via useSelector (reactive) or direct store access
(imperative/animation loops)."

The Technical "Why" (Succinct Explanation):
React Hooks (useState) couple your data's lifecycle to the Component's render cycle. This is fatal for high-performance or complex apps because:
1. The "Stale Closure" Trap: React components are functions that capture variables at a specific moment in time. In a requestAnimationFrame loop
   (running at 60fps), a closure created by useEffect or useCallback often sees "old" data from the previous render, causing logic bugs that are
   nearly impossible to debug.
2. Async/Batching De-sync: React state updates are asynchronous and batched. If you set state in Frame A, you might not read it back until Frame
   B. In game logic, you need the value immediately in the same tick.
3. Performance Checkmate: To fix stale closures, you add dependencies to useEffect. This forces the component to re-render constantly just to
   "refresh" the closure, killing performance.
4. Serialization: Hooks state is locked inside the component tree. You cannot easily snapshot it, persist it to local storage, or send it over a
   WebSocket for multiplayer sync without writing complex "extraction" logic. External stores are serialized by default.

  ---

Part 2: Critical Audit of @zazcart/tools State Machine

I have analyzed your code in packages/toolkit/src/state-machine.
Verdict: It is a robust, feature-rich library for Business Apps, but it has critical performance bottlenecks for High-Frequency 3D/Game
scenarios.

Here is the analysis of 95% of the aspects involved.

1. Architecture & Design
* The Good:
    * Architecture: The "Slice" pattern combined with useSyncExternalStore is the correct modern approach.
    * Dev Experience: Usage of Immer simplifies reducer logic significantly.
    * Hydration: The built-in Cache and Metadata strategies are excellent for "Resume where I left off" features in business apps.
* The Bad (Architecture):
    * Over-abstraction: You have built a wrapper around Redux principles but added significant runtime overhead for features (like Metadata)
      that are not always needed.

2. Performance Bottlenecks (The "Killer" issues)
* Garbage Collection Thrashing (`machine.ts`):
    * Problem: The UpsertStateMetadata function runs on every single action dispatch. It spreads a new object: [METADATA_FIELD_NAME]: {
      ...previousMetadata, changes: changes + 1, ... }.
    * Impact: In a 3D game dragging an object (dispatching MOVE 60 times/sec), you are allocating 60 new metadata objects per second, plus the
      root state object. This will trigger the Garbage Collector frequently, causing "stutters" (frame drops).
* Immer Overhead:
    * Problem: You use produce (Immer) inside RootReducer and effectively wrap selectors in GetSelectorsWrapper.
    * Impact: Immer is fast, but it uses Proxies. Wrapping/Unwrapping proxies 60 times a second adds measurable overhead compared to raw object
      spread or direct mutation (in controlled environments).
* Runtime Validation (`context.ts`):
    * Problem: In context.ts, the Handler.get() calls check() which calls Assert which validates a schema.
    * Code: runtimeActions in hooks.ts calls getContext() on every dispatch.
    * Impact: Every time you dispatch an action, you are paying the cost of a schema validation check. This is unnecessary CPU waste in
      production.

3. Specific Code Issues & Bugs
* The "Hybrid Object" Anti-Pattern (`hooks.ts`):
    * Line: return Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection);
    * Analysis: You are returning an Array that is also an Object (with properties).
    * Why it's bad: V8 (Chrome's JS engine) hates this. It creates a complex "shape" that de-optimizes property access. It makes JIT compilation
      harder. Just return an object { state, actions } or a tuple [state, actions]. Don't mix them.
* Double Proxying (`machine.ts`):
    * Code: GetSelectorsWrapper uses produce(state, ...) to inject selector values.
    * Analysis: You are running Immer after the reducer has already run. You are double-processing the state tree.
* Selector Equality:
    * Analysis: Your store notifies listeners on any change. useSyncExternalStore handles the "select" logic, but if you have expensive
      selectors derived from state (e.g., filtering a list of 10,000 items), your current implementation re-runs the selector on every dispatch
      of any action, even if the relevant slice didn't change. You lack "memoized selectors" (like reselect).

4. Recommendation for your 3D Game Use Case

Your current library is too heavy for the onUpdate / useFrame loop of a 3D engine.

Immediate Fixes for `packages/toolkit`:
1. Bypass Validation: Add a flag to disable context.ts schema validation in production (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production').
2. Opt-out Metadata: Allow creating a machine with metadata: false. A MOUSE_MOVE action should not update updatedAt timestamps or change
   counters.
3. Optimize Hooks: Change the return type of useMachine to a standard object or tuple. Remove Object.assign magic.

Alternative Approach:
For the 3D transforms, do not use this library's dispatch loop.
* Keep: User Profile, Inventory, UI State in this library.
* Discard: Mouse coordinates, Camera rotation, Object positions. Use a raw Float32Array or a simple ref based mutable store (like
  valtio/vanilla) for those specific high-frequency values to bypass the Redux/Immer/Metadata overhead completely.

Summary
The library is "Enterprise Grade" (good for reliable business logic, hydration, caching) but "Game Engine Unfriendly" (allocates too much memory
per tick).

content.content:
1→# FIXME: State Machine Audit & Performance Analysis
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→The `@zazcart/tools` state machine is architecturally sound for standard enterprise applications (forms, dashboards, low-frequency updates) but **critically inefficient** for high-frequency scenarios like 3D Game Loops (60FPS+).
     5→
     6→## Critical Issues (Performance Killers)
     7→
     8→### 1. Garbage Collection Thrashing (`machine.ts`)
     9→- **Location:** `UpsertStateMetadata` function.
    10→- **Problem:** Every single action dispatch creates a NEW metadata object:
    11→  ```typescript
    12→  [METADATA_FIELD_NAME]: { ...previousMetadata, changes: changes + 1, ... }
    13→  ```
    14→- **Impact:** In a game loop (e.g., `MOUSE_MOVE`), this allocates ~60 objects/second just for metadata. This triggers frequent GC pauses ("stuttering").
    15→- **Fix:** Implement an opt-out mechanism (`metadata: false`) for high-frequency slices.
    16→
    17→### 2. Validation on Hot Path (`context.ts` / `hooks.ts`)
    18→- **Location:** `context.get()` calls `check()` -> `Assert()`.
    19→- **Problem:** Every action dispatch validates the Context schema.
    20→- **Impact:** Unnecessary CPU cycles spent checking invariants that rarely change.
    21→- **Fix:**
    22→  - Wrap validations in `if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')`.
    23→  - Disable context checks entirely for specific "Game Mode" machines.
    24→
    25→### 3. V8 De-optimization (`hooks.ts`)
    26→- **Location:** `useMachine` return value.
    27→- **Code:**
    28→  ```typescript
    29→  return Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection);
    30→  ```
    31→- **Problem:** Returning a "Hybrid Object" (Array + Object properties) changes the internal "Hidden Class" (Map) of the object in V8. This severely de-optimizes property access and prevents efficient JIT compilation.
    32→- **Fix:** Return a standard Tuple or Object.
    33→  ```typescript
    34→  return { state, actions }; // OR [state, actions]
    35→  ```
    36→
    37→### 4. Double Proxying Overhead
    38→- **Location:** `RootReducer` + `GetSelectorsWrapper`.
    39→- **Problem:** `Immer` (Proxies) is used in the reducer, and then potentially again in selectors.
    40→- **Impact:** Proxy wrapping/unwrapping is expensive.
    41→- **Fix:** Ensure selectors run on raw objects where possible, or use memoized selectors (like `reselect`) to avoid re-computation on every dispatch.
    42→
    43→### 5. Unsafe Listener Unsubscription (`store.ts`)
    44→- **Location:** `dispatch` loop vs `unsubscribe`.
    45→- **Problem:** The `dispatch` function iterates over `currentListeners` directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, `currentListeners.splice` mutates the array *while* it is being iterated.
    46→- **Impact:** Can cause listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.
    47→- **Fix:** Copy the array before iterating, or use a linked list.
    48→  ```typescript
    49→  const listeners = currentListeners.slice();

# FIXME: State Machine Audit & Performance Analysis

## Overview
The `@zazcart/tools` state machine is architecturally sound for standard enterprise applications (forms, dashboards, low-frequency updates) but **critically inefficient** for high-frequency scenarios like 3D Game Loops (60FPS+).

## Critical Issues (Performance Killers)

### 1. Garbage Collection Thrashing (`machine.ts`)
- **Location:** `UpsertStateMetadata` function.
- **Problem:** Every single action dispatch creates a NEW metadata object:
  ```typescript
  [METADATA_FIELD_NAME]: { ...previousMetadata, changes: changes + 1, ... }
  ```
- **Impact:** In a game loop (e.g., `MOUSE_MOVE`), this allocates ~60 objects/second just for metadata. This triggers frequent GC pauses ("stuttering").
- **Fix:** Implement an opt-out mechanism (`metadata: false`) for high-frequency slices.

### 2. Validation on Hot Path (`context.ts` / `hooks.ts`)
- **Location:** `context.get()` calls `check()` -> `Assert()`.
- **Problem:** Every action dispatch validates the Context schema.
- **Impact:** Unnecessary CPU cycles spent checking invariants that rarely change.
- **Fix:**
  - Wrap validations in `if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')`.
  - Disable context checks entirely for specific "Game Mode" machines.

### 3. V8 De-optimization (`hooks.ts`)
- **Location:** `useMachine` return value.
- **Code:**
  ```typescript
  return Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection);
  ```
- **Problem:** Returning a "Hybrid Object" (Array + Object properties) changes the internal "Hidden Class" (Map) of the object in V8. This severely de-optimizes property access and prevents efficient JIT compilation.
- **Fix:** Return a standard Tuple or Object.
  ```typescript
  return { state, actions }; // OR [state, actions]
  ```

### 4. Double Proxying Overhead
- **Location:** `RootReducer` + `GetSelectorsWrapper`.
- **Problem:** `Immer` (Proxies) is used in the reducer, and then potentially again in selectors.
- **Impact:** Proxy wrapping/unwrapping is expensive.
- **Fix:** Ensure selectors run on raw objects where possible, or use memoized selectors (like `reselect`) to avoid re-computation on every dispatch.

### 5. Unsafe Listener Unsubscription (`store.ts`)
- **Location:** `dispatch` loop vs `unsubscribe`.
- **Problem:** The `dispatch` function iterates over `currentListeners` directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, `currentListeners.splice` mutates the array *while* it is being iterated.
- **Impact:** Can cause listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.
- **Fix:** Copy the array before iterating, or use a linked list.
  ```typescript
  const listeners = currentListeners.slice();
  for (const listener of listeners) { ... }
  ```

## Architectural Recommendations

### "Anti-Hooks" Doctrine
- **Keep:** Using this Store for "Slow" State (UI, Inventory, Profile, Settings).
- **Discard:** Using this Store for "Fast" State (Entity Transforms, Physics, Camera).
- **Strategy:** For the 3D Engine, use `Float32Array`, `Three.js` objects, or simple Mutable Classes. Do **NOT** route frame-by-frame logic through `dispatch()`.

## Action Plan
- [ ] Add `metadata: boolean` flag to `MachineConfig`.
- [ ] Strip `context.ts` validation in Production builds.
- [ ] Refactor `useMachine` to return a stable shape (Object or Tuple).
- [ ] Optimize `selector` implementation to avoid running on every unrelated action.

// 'Sep 25' 658→ 659→// Set global default locale 660→import { ptBR } from 'date-fns/locale'; 661→setDefaultDateLocale(ptBR); 662→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM'); // 'dezembro' 663→ 664→// Override locale for single call 665→import { enUS } from 'date-fns/locale'; 1→# Harness Dashboard - Progress Log
     2→
     3→## Project Goal
     4→Web interface for managing multiple Claude Code instances with real-time monitoring, task management (Kanban/Graph), chat UI, and Anti-Go-Horse Protocol enforcement.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Session 1 - 2025-12-16 - Project Setup & Harness Initialization
     9→
    10→### Context
    11→User requested a web dashboard for the harness-orchestrator with:
    12→- React + shadcn/ui 2 + Astro.js + MongoDB local
    13→- Slack-like chat UI where agents are "people"
    14→- Debug panel for terminal output (separate from chat)
    15→- Kanban/mindmap for task management with parallel execution

# Harness Dashboard - Progress Log

## Project Goal
Web interface for managing multiple Claude Code instances with real-time monitoring, task management (Kanban/Graph), chat UI, and Anti-Go-Horse Protocol enforcement.

---

## Session 1 - 2025-12-16 - Project Setup & Harness Initialization

### Context
User requested a web dashboard for the harness-orchestrator with:
- React + shadcn/ui 2 + Astro.js + MongoDB local
- Slack-like chat UI where agents are "people"
- Debug panel for terminal output (separate from chat)
- Kanban/mindmap for task management with parallel execution
- Anti-Go-Horse Protocol (AGPH) to prevent AI loops
- Full parity with file-based harness (auditable via UI)
- Self-hosting: this project uses its own harness during development

### Completed ✅

1. **Created Plan** (deep thinking mode)
   - Explored harness-orchestrator patterns
   - Explored @zazcart/tools utilities
   - Explored main project harness protocol
   - Designed 32 features across 8 phases

2. **Created CLAUDE.md**
   - Project overview and tech stack
   - Harness protocol instructions
   - Anti-Go-Horse Protocol (AGPH) with 4 escalation levels
   - Code conventions and dependencies policy

3. **Created features.json**
   - 32 features (D001-D032)
   - 8 phases: Setup → Backend → UI → Tasks → AI → Polish → Parity → AGPH
   - Full dependency graph with blockedBy

4. **Created progress.txt** (this file)

### Architecture Decisions

#### Tech Stack
| Technology | Choice | Reason |
|------------|--------|--------|
| Framework | Astro 5 | SSG + React islands |
| UI | React 19 + shadcn/ui 2 | Modern, accessible components |
| Styling | Tailwind 4 | Fast, utility-first |
| Database | MongoDB 6 (native) | No mongoose (user request) |
| Validation | @zazcart/tools schema | Reuse existing |
| HTTP | Hono 4 | Fast, TypeScript-first |
| WebSocket | ws 8 | Real-time updates |
| Graph | react-flow 11 | Dependency visualization |

#### Anti-Go-Horse Protocol (AGPH)
Prevents AI agents from looping on errors:
- Level 1: Same error 2x → PAUSE + review
- Level 2: Same file edited 3x → SEARCH web
- Level 3: Build fails 3x → ESCALATE (research/help)
- Level 4: No solution → STOP + notify human

#### Harness Parity
All file-based harness features mapped to UI:
- features.json → Kanban Board + Graph View
- progress.txt → Timeline + Session History
- CLAUDE.md → Agent Config Editor
- .claude/hooks/ → Hook Editor + Logs
- settings.json → Permission Matrix

### Files Created This Session

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | AI instructions with AGPH |
| `features.json` | 32 features definition |
| `progress.txt` | This file |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Hook registration (pending) |
| `.claude/hooks/` | Hook scripts (pending) |

### Current State

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| D001 | 🔓 Ready | Package init (NEXT) |
| D002-D004 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs D001 |
| D005-D032 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs earlier features |

### Parallelization Analysis
After D001 completes, these can run in parallel:
- D002 (MongoDB)
- D003 (WebSocket)
- D004 (Agents structure)
- D009 (Layout shell)

This is a 4x speedup opportunity!

### Next Session Priorities

1. **Complete harness setup**: Create .claude/ hooks
2. **D001**: Initialize Astro + React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui

## 🔄 Trabalho em Andamento Encontrado

Encontrei **2 iniciativas** pendentes:

---

### 1. **Auditoria de Performance do @zazcart/tools** 🔴 CRÍTICO

Arquivos FIXME documentam problemas identificados mas **NÃO corrigidos**:

| Problema | Arquivo | Impacto |
|----------|---------|---------|
| **GC Thrashing** | `machine.ts` | 60 objetos/s criados em game loops |
| **Validação no Hot Path** | `context.ts` | CPU desperdiçada em checks desnecessários |
| **V8 De-optimization** | `hooks.ts` | `Object.assign([...], ...)` quebra JIT |
| **Double Proxying** | `machine.ts` | Immer roda 2x no mesmo state |
| **Unsafe Unsubscribe** | `store.ts` | Listeners podem ser pulados |

**Action Items pendentes:**
- [ ] Add `metadata: boolean` flag to `MachineConfig`
- [ ] Strip `context.ts` validation in Production builds
- [ ] Refactor `useMachine` to return a stable shape (Object or Tuple)
- [ ] Optimize `selector` implementation

---

### 2. **harness-dashboard** (packages/harness-dashboard)

Projeto iniciado mas **D001** (package init) ainda não completado:
- 32 features planejadas (D001-D032)
- Stack: Astro 5 + React 19 + shadcn/ui 2 + MongoDB
- Anti-Go-Horse Protocol (AGPH) documentado

---

### 3. **Arquivos não commitados**

21 arquivos modificados não commitados (ver git status acima)

---

1. **Continuar auditoria @zazcart/tools** → Implementar correções do FIXME
2. **Continuar harness-dashboard** → Iniciar D001 (package init)

4. **Outro** → Especifique
